title: Brachyphragma mohavense
taxonomy: Brachyphragma mohavense is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It is a parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by attaching itself to the roots of other plants. This rare and endangered species is native to the Mojave Desert in California, USA. Brachyphragma mohavense is considered a unique and fascinating example of the diverse range of plant life found in the desert ecosystems of North America. Further classification of Brachyphragma mohavense includes its placement in the genus Brachyphragma.

intro: Brachyphragma mohavense, also known as Mohave desert lavender, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. The bioactive constituents of Brachyphragma mohavense include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties.
